data.description:
## Why
The new impact models change scoring significantly. Following the proven plan-review playbook (ship
advisory/permissive, dogfood, then calibrate), we roll out permissive, accumulate a field corpus, and
tune the blocking thresholds down only when data + an A/B justify it.

## What
- Ship both redesigned impact models behind PERMISSIVE thresholds (no new hard blocks at rollout).
- Ensure both gates emit REVIEW_RESULT sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers +
  `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint` join keys (code-review via the data-capture child).
- Provide the offline replay calibration (extend `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`)
  and a diff-grounded A/B harness that scores the labeled/adjudicated set with the new model and reports
  separation, gating any threshold-down change on beating the current model.

## Scope
- Config flags for permissive thresholds; sidecar field additions; calibration/replay + A/B scripts and
  a documented tuning cadence. Depends on the two impact-redesign children and the data-capture child.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New impact models ship behind permissive thresholds (no new hard blocks at rollout).
- [ ] Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys.
- [ ] An offline replay + diff-grounded A/B report measures separation of block-worthy vs nit on the
      adjudicated set and gates any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model.
- [ ] A documented recalibration cadence (as the corpus grows, conditioned on model) is recorded.
data.title: Permissive rollout + calibration harness for the new impact models
